title: Billing Suspension
description: Invoice shows paid but the container host is still suspended; customer is frustrated and wants ownership.

ticket.subject: Invoice paid, but site still suspended
recentIncidents: Invoice #HB-4927 cleared 17 minutes ago but suspension flag persists
Customer reports the panel still shows 'Service Suspended'

thread.author: Morgan (customer)
thread.message: I paid the invoice through Stripe. Why is the hosting still suspended?

thread.author: Lena Torres
thread.message: Thanks for the screenshot of the receipt. I'm checking with billing ops before unsuspending.

internalNotes.author: Ops Lead
internalNotes.message: Billing clearing webhook is delayed; invoice is marked paid but suspension lock still on database.

postmortem.rootCause: Suspension flag not cleared after manual payment reconciliation.
postmortem.fix: Cleared the suspension flag and re-ran the notification job.
postmortem.followUp: Notify billing automation to recheck the webhook within 15 minutes.
postmortem.prevention: Add coverage to the reconciliation job to release suspensions when invoices hit paid state.

metrics.label: SLA compliance
metrics.note: Escalated before SLA breached but still under 60-minute target.

metrics.label: Communication & empathy
metrics.note: Customer-facing reply acknowledged pain but lacked next ETA.

metrics.label: Technical ownership
metrics.note: Internal note captured the automation delay.

metrics.label: KB & self-service
metrics.note: Shared billing hold article; could customize further.

metrics.label: Closure completeness
metrics.note: Postmortem ready but still needs verification.

escalationRules.notes: Auto-escalation kicks in 45 minutes after first response; Finance gets CC when invoice states mismatch.

title: Minecraft Plugin Crash
description: New modpack plugin causes the dedicated host to crash at startup, blocking the customer's community.

ticket.subject: Server crashes when the new economy plugin loads
recentIncidents: Crash logs reference ModEconomy v3.2 failing sanity check
Server reboots when plugin tries to bind to local database

thread.author: Imani (customer)
thread.message: After I installed the new economy plugin, the server won't start.

internalNotes.author: Ravi
internalNotes.message: Resembles ModEconomy conflict with the custom Harmony patch. Container logs show stack overflow after plugin init.

postmortem.rootCause: Harmony patch conflicted with ModEconomy v3.2, leading to repeated stack overflows.
postmortem.fix: Rolled back to ModEconomy v3.0 and patched Harmony to respect new APIs.
postmortem.followUp: Recommend plugin compatibility check before deploying any new mod on this host.
postmortem.prevention: Add a mod compatibility checklist to the KB and rerun automated sanity tests.

metrics.label: SLA compliance
metrics.note: SLA is tight but response time was 5 minutes.

metrics.label: Communication & empathy
metrics.note: Acknowledged outage but can add step-by-step updates.

metrics.label: Technical ownership
metrics.note: Internal note documents stack trace details.

metrics.label: KB & self-service
metrics.note: Sharing compatibility article would calm the gaming guild.

metrics.label: Escalation judgment
metrics.note: Ticket stays Tier 1 but has path to Tier 2.

escalationRules.notes: Tier 1 should collect crash dumps before involving engineering.

title: Discord Bot Overnight Outage
description: Discord moderation bot hosted on HostDesk went offline overnight; premium customer needs postmortem.

ticket.subject: Bot went offline between 03:00 and 05:00
recentIncidents: External monitoring recorded 120 minutes of downtime
Bot restarted automatically but core listeners did not reconnect

thread.author: Mira (customer)
thread.message: Discord moderators were unable to run commands while the bot was down.

thread.author: Noah
thread.message: I'm digging into the approximate outage window; can you confirm if any manual restarts occurred?

internalNotes.author: Noah
internalNotes.message: App server crashed when Discord API hits exceeded the user tier; listener thread failed to reregister.

postmortem.rootCause: Rate-limit spike triggered automatic restarts but the reconnection loop never finished.
postmortem.fix: Restarted the discord bot with throttled re-registration and force update on the API token.
postmortem.followUp: Revisit premium tier concurrency limits and note it in the incident report.
postmortem.prevention: Add jittered back-off to the listener and monitor the queue depth.

metrics.label: SLA compliance
metrics.note: Responded within 5 minutes; SLA still open.

metrics.label: Communication & empathy
metrics.note: Clear question asked, but needs follow-up summary.

metrics.label: Technical ownership
metrics.note: Internal note captured the failure window.

metrics.label: KB & self-service
metrics.note: Incident article ready to share once validated.

metrics.label: Escalation judgment
metrics.note: Elevated due to premium plan SLA.

escalationRules.notes: Escalate immediately if outages exceed 30 minutes rollout.

title: cPanel Login Loop
description: Customer cannot break out of the cPanel login loop after repeated 2FA failures; they fear an attack.

ticket.subject: cPanel keeps kicking me back to the login screen
recentIncidents: Two-factor auth request attempted unsuccessfully four times
Browser shows login success but returns to the same form

thread.author: Ava (customer)
thread.message: I keep entering the code and it refreshes me back to the login screen.

internalNotes.author: Jules
internalNotes.message: Artifact from SSO session cookie; reissuing cookie should fix loop.

postmortem.rootCause: SSO cookie had expired token, causing redirect loop.
postmortem.fix: Cleared cPanel session and forced new SSO handshake.
postmortem.followUp: Document the cookie refresh steps for frontline staff.
postmortem.prevention: Add a KB snippet on cookie refresh to the login article.

metrics.label: SLA compliance
metrics.note: Within SLA window but earlier response could reduce stress.

metrics.label: Communication & empathy
metrics.note: Calm tone but can offer more assurance.

escalationRules.notes: Escalate if OTP requests exceed 4 in a 30-minute window.

title: Backup Restore Failure
description: Customer ran the restore button but their database stays at the pre-rollback state.

ticket.subject: Restored backup but site still old version
recentIncidents: Flat-file backup flagged 7 minutes after restore
Database restore job completed but elapsed 2 hours ago

thread.author: Emir (customer)
thread.message: I ran the restore for the Feb 10 backup but I still see the older homepage.

internalNotes.author: Imani
internalNotes.message: Restore completed but caching layer still pointing to cached HTML. Need to flush CDN.

postmortem.rootCause: CDN cache and database were not invalidated after restore.
postmortem.fix: Ran cache flush and validated file timestamps.
postmortem.followUp: Add note to run CDN purge automatically after restores for this tier.
postmortem.prevention: Document the CDN flush step in the restore KB article.

metrics.label: SLA compliance
metrics.note: Still inside the longer SLA window.

metrics.label: Communication & empathy
metrics.note: Needs more clarity on the next steps.

escalationRules.notes: Escalate if backup restore exceeds 3 hours or CDN flush fails twice.

title: DNS Change Not Propagating
description: DNS change landed in registrars but the domain still points to the old host across Europe.

ticket.subject: DNS record update not propagating
recentIncidents: TTL for A record is 900 seconds but 4 hours later old IP still resolves in EU
Registrar confirms the update completed, but the NS group still shows old entry

thread.author: Sofia (customer)
thread.message: The site still loads the old server despite updating the DNS.

internalNotes.author: Evelyn
internalNotes.message: Current NS set includes legacy overlay; plan to push new NS group and monitor.

postmortem.rootCause: Legacy NS group slowed propagation across the European edge nodes.
postmortem.fix: Updated NS group, confirmed propagation in 10 minutes, and cleared CDN caches.
postmortem.followUp: Add stub to KB about checking registrar NS after hot changes.
postmortem.prevention: Prep DNS change checklist with propagation monitoring steps.

metrics.label: SLA compliance
metrics.note: Response came before SLA window closed.

metrics.label: Communication & empathy
metrics.note: Customer feels heard but needs propagation updates.

metrics.label: Escalation judgment
metrics.note: Plan not yet to push to Tier 2.

customerProfile.name: Atlas Artisan
customerProfile.persona: Creative marketer with limited DNS knowledge

escalationRules.notes: Escalate if propagation exceeds 6 hours or registrar denies changes.

title: High Ping / Lag Complaint
description: Customer sees constant 300+ ms ping spikes during peak night hours despite premium plan.

ticket.subject: Players seeing 300ms latency spikes in the evening
recentIncidents: Peak load starts at 20:00 UTC, coinciding with player queue growth
Host CPU spikes to 95% when latency climbs

thread.author: Irene (customer)
thread.message: Saw 300ms ping tonight, the community is noticing huge frame drops.

internalNotes.author: Nia
internalNotes.message: Host metrics show CPU climb; suspect cron job spike affecting IO.

postmortem.rootCause: Nightly backup cron job contended with game server threads, spiking latency.
postmortem.fix: Rescheduled backups and added CPU guard rails.
postmortem.followUp: Add automation to pause non-critical jobs during peak concurrency.
postmortem.prevention: Document the CPU scheduling guidelines in the performance KB.

metrics.label: Communication & empathy
metrics.note: Took ownership but follow-up promises needed.

escalationRules.notes: Offer to escalate when lag persists for two business hours.

title: Mod Conflict
description: Two mods try to write to the same file, causing a startup failure and a crash dump for the server.

ticket.subject: Modpack crashes with stack trace pointing to textures.json
recentIncidents: Crash occurs shortly after the world loads
Textures module can't find textures.json when both mods install

thread.author: Kara (customer)
thread.message: We hit a crash while loading the modpack after the latest update.

internalNotes.author: Ravi
internalNotes.message: Mods A and B both write textures.json; need to modify the override order.

postmortem.rootCause: Two textures mods fighting over the same file when the world loads.
postmortem.fix: Temporarily disabled the secondary mod and documented the override order.
postmortem.followUp: Recommend mod compatibility check before patching next update.
postmortem.prevention: Add mod conflict detection to automation.

metrics.label: SLA compliance
metrics.note: Fast reply; still in SLA window.

escalationRules.notes: Escalate if a repeat mod conflict occurs after two reorders.

title: Upgrade Request Without Data Loss
description: Customer wants to move from Standard to Pro without downtime and needs assurance on migration steps.

ticket.subject: Requesting upgrade to Pro tier without losing configs
recentIncidents: Customer sees the Pro tier listed but upgrade button is disabled
No recent failures; just planning quote

thread.author: Gaby (customer)
thread.message: Can we upgrade to Pro but keep the same IP and configs?

internalNotes.author: Lena
internalNotes.message: Plan change requires a short downtime to scale storage. Need to coordinate with billing ops.

metrics.label: SLA compliance
metrics.note: Still early in queue for planning request.

metrics.label: Technical ownership
metrics.note: Need to confirm storage scaling steps.

escalationRules.notes: Escalate if upgrade requires manual provisioning longer than 4 hours.

title: Downgrade & Refund Question

ticket.subject: Need to downgrade and refund unused days
recentIncidents: Customer requests downgrade to Standard
Mentions budget hold until next quarter

thread.author: Theo (customer)
thread.message: Can I downgrade to Standard and get a refund for the weeks left?

internalNotes.author: Lena
internalNotes.message: Refund policy allows prorated credit; need finance approval for early downgrade.

postmortem.followUp: Prepare downgrade instructions for future CFO inquiries.
postmortem.prevention: Add plan comparison to billing KB.

metrics.label: SLA compliance
metrics.note: Standard SLA window for billing questions.

metrics.label: Communication & empathy

metrics.label: Technical ownership
metrics.note: More clarity on plan differences needed.

escalationRules.notes: Escalate to finance for any refunds that exceed $1,000.

title: Domain Renewal Confusion
description: Customer says domain expired despite auto-renew being on; they want clarity before renewal hits their card.

ticket.subject: Domain renewal charged twice
recentIncidents: Wake up to charge from registrar despite auto-renew on
Registrar support assigned ticket #REG-908

thread.author: Elise (customer)
thread.message: I was billed twice for the domain and now it's showing past due.

internalNotes.author: Lena
internalNotes.message: Registrar report says auto-renew failed due to expired card; need to coordinate refund for duplicate charge.

postmortem.rootCause: Failed card prevented auto-renew so registrar issued manual charge.
postmortem.fix: Processed refund for duplicate charge and updated card on file.
postmortem.followUp: Send confirmation of renewal status once registrar updates records.
postmortem.prevention: Refresh billing KB to call out manual renewal steps.

metrics.label: Communication & empathy
metrics.note: Needs more empathy on double charge.

escalationRules.notes: Escalate to registrar team if invoice shows past due for more than 24 hours.

title: Failed Autopay, Active Card
description: Autopay bounced despite an active card; customer worries service may suspend even though payment is ready.

ticket.subject: Autopay failed even though card is valid
recentIncidents: Authorization error 402 returned from gateway
Card last used two days ago successfully

thread.author: Elliot (customer)
thread.message: My autopay failed, but the card is still active. Will service get suspended?

internalNotes.author: Mara
internalNotes.message: Gateway sent 402 but the card response indicates AVS mismatch; need to verify address.

postmortem.rootCause: Autopay retried after AVS mismatch; system queued service hold.
postmortem.fix: Reauthorized card manually with updated billing address.
postmortem.followUp: Document the AVS issue in autopay KB.
postmortem.prevention: Add a 2-step verification before triggering suspension.

escalationRules.notes: Escalate if autopay fails twice or service shows pending suspension.

title: Angry Customer After Repeated Outages
description: Customer is irate after multiple brief outages this week and expects ownership.

ticket.subject: Outages keep happening, this is unacceptable
recentIncidents: Three short outages in past eight hours
Customer manual downtime reports trending up

thread.author: Nora (customer)
thread.message: I've already opened four tickets and still no resolution.

internalNotes.author: Noah
internalNotes.message: Outages align with nightly patch window; need better expectation-setting.

postmortem.rootCause: Patch window overlapped with peak usage, resulting in multiple restarts.
postmortem.fix: Shifted patch window and proactively notified the team.
postmortem.followUp: Offer loyalty gesture and proactive monitoring summary.

metrics.label: SLA compliance
metrics.note: Immediate acknowledgment but requires faster updates.

metrics.label: Communication & empathy
metrics.note: Strong empathy but needs root cause clarity.

escalationRules.notes: Leadership notified if outages repeat three times in a day.

title: Unsupported Admin Action Request
description: Customer asks for unsupported command permissions; the agent must say no while offering alternatives.

ticket.subject: Can you give me root shell access to install custom kernel?
recentIncidents: Customer insists custom kernel is needed for legacy mod
Customer aware it's outside supported scope

thread.author: Leo (customer)
thread.message: Can you enable root access so I can install my custom kernel?

internalNotes.author: Jules
internalNotes.message: Policy forbids root shell; suggest container layering instead.

postmortem.followUp: Provide KB guidance about container layering.
postmortem.prevention: Link policy KB to the request form.

metrics.label: SLA compliance
metrics.note: Standard SLA window for policy questions.

escalationRules.notes: Policy team reviews any request for privileged access.

Customer calls urgent without details

thread.author: Customer
thread.message: My site is broken, fix now!

postmortem.prevention: Link diagnostics checklist in the reply.

recentIncidents: Customer referencing faster responses from another provider
Ticket mentions upcoming launch in 12 hours

title: Duplicate Tickets from Same Incident
description: Multiple tickets opened for the same outage; triage must merge and de-escalate.

ticket.subject: Issue still happening (duplicate)
recentIncidents: Original outage ticket already in Tier 2
Customer opened duplicate with similar description

thread.author: Customer
thread.message: Just checking back, issue still happening.

internalNotes.author: Mara
internalNotes.message: Link to ticket #5123 and merge updates in original thread.

postmortem.rootCause: Customer not sure whether their first ticket dropped off.
postmortem.followUp: Remind them to reference the ongoing incident moving forward.
postmortem.prevention: Add guidance on duplicate reporting to KB.

metrics.label: Escalation judgment
metrics.note: Should stay at Tier 1 and reference Tier 2 ticket.

escalationRules.notes: Merge duplicates before escalating; only escalate if first ticket needs Tier 2.
